Strip_Summary: First-person view seated in underground room. Sixteen glowing screens line the wall.
Stickman in situation room  -  one screen shows burning facility. Order given 47 minutes ago.
Stickman staring at screen  -  smoke spreading sideways like something alive.
Stickman rigid in chair  -  recycled air and cold coffee. Someone clears throat behind. He doesn't turn.
Split concept  -  stickman selling apartments (left) vs stickman at war table (right). Same math.
Bold white text on dark background: 'Except nobody signs at the end.'
Stickman descending golden escalator in gleaming tower lobby  -  cameras flashing below, confident wave.
Bold white text on dark background: 'You're sure of it.'
Small stickman boy in large house  -  father in hard hat, brick apartment buildings, outer borough.
Bold white text on dark background: 'But the money is good. The money is always good.'
Boy stickman at construction site on Saturday  -  oversized hard hat wobbling on small head.
Father stickman pointing at rising building  -  teaching never show weakness. Boy doesn't understand yet.
Close-up of small stickman hands gripping hard hat strap under chin. Sensory memory detail.
Boy stickman in military cadet uniform  -  academy upstate, mother crying at door, father stoic.
Stickman in uniform standing in barracks hallway  -  discipline doesn't fit yet. Learning something.
Stickman lying awake in dormitory bunk  -  rows of beds, floor wax smell, forty boys breathing.
Bold white text on dark background: 'How to own a room before anyone speaks.'
Stickman in cadet posture  -  straight spine, loud voice. Lesson absorbed for sixty years.
Stickman at military academy looking toward city skyline  -  thinks this is about real estate.
Young stickman in graduation gown  -  diploma on wall, war newsreel on TV, draft papers on desk.
Stickman holding medical deferment paper  -  bone spurs notation. Doesn't remember which foot.
Bold white text on dark background: 'You never talk about it. Not then. Not later.'
Stickman receiving company keys from father  -  looking past outer borough toward Manhattan skyline.
Vertical split  -  left: brick buildings and parking lots. Right: tallest towers on earth. Everything father never reached.
Stickman gesturing confidently  -  build bigger, talk louder. Weight line: 'You don't.'
Tall stickman in dark suit and red tie  -  tower opens on most famous avenue. Age thirty-seven.
Gold lobby, gold elevators  -  stickman's name five stories tall in gold letters. People stop to look up.
Stickman standing still before tower  -  chasing this feeling for the rest of his life. Warm amber glow.
Stickman on magazine cover, private helicopter overhead  -  symbol of a decade, ambition without apology.
Bold white text on dark background: 'You don't care which.'
Stickman standing on casino floor  -  three casinos in gambling town by shore, biggest in world.
Casino interior  -  debt piling higher than chips. Slot machines and empty chairs.
Casino floor at midnight  -  slot machines pinging, tourists losing borrowed money, ceiling camera watching.
Stickman at desk with bankruptcy paperwork  -  three times corporate. Name still on buildings.
Stickman before world map of towers with his name  -  pins worldwide, owns nothing inside any of them.
Bold white text on dark background: 'But the name is still five stories tall.'
Stickman surviving wreckage  -  name floats above buildings like separate entity. License the brand.
Towers rising worldwide with stickman's name  -  doesn't own single brick inside any of them.
Stickman mid-sentence trying to explain how the name works  -  gives up. It doesn't come out right.
Bold white text on dark background: 'You stop trying after that.'
Stickman behind desk pointing at contestants  -  TV studio cameras, fourteen seasons, national brand.
Bold white text on dark background: 'You're a brand. And brands don't go bankrupt.'
Stickman riding golden escalator down in own tower  -  cameras below, announcing run for highest office.
Bold white text on dark background: 'No experience. No credentials. No apology.'
Row of TV talking heads laughing  -  then falling silent as stickman appears at victory podium.
Bold white text on dark background: 'Because you win.'
Stickman at seventy walking into executive office  -  presidential seal on carpet.
Stickman pressing butler button under desk  -  man in white jacket appears with silver tray. Testing power.
Stickman ordering diet soda in most powerful room on earth  -  used the button just for a drink.
Oval Office interior  -  resolute desk, presidential seal, the weight of the room vs the triviality of the moment.
Stickman at podium tearing up document on live television  -  eight flags behind, cameras recording.
Torn paper pieces falling  -  worst deal ever, will get better one. No replacement in sight.
Stickman at desk reviewing sanctions data  -  maximum pressure policy, layer after layer.
Briefing page showing adversary economy collapsing  -  oil exports near zero, currency crashed, hospitals empty.
Stickman reading briefing numbers  -  imports down, reserves depleted. Numbers going right direction.
Stickman at desk at 3AM  -  drone feed on screen showing road explosion near airport. Commander eliminated.
Region holds breath  -  stickman posting on phone before official statement. Jaw tight.
Stickman touching jaw  -  tension building. Not sure when that started.
Stickman standing between two leaders on mansion lawn  -  normalization signing ceremony.
Handshakes and flags of four nations  -  peace through strength on the lawn.
Stickman at rally podium, crowd roaring  -  adversary isolated, ally stronger. Believes it.
Bold white text on dark background: 'You're sure of it.'
Laboratory across ocean  -  centrifuge spinning faster. Stickman doesn't know that yet.
News broadcast  -  January 2021 headline, legislature building under siege.
Stickman watching TV  -  crowd breaking windows, sitting in chairs, one carrying flag with his name.
Bold white text on dark background: 'You don't call it off. Not immediately.'
Stickman at impeachment proceedings  -  second time. Acquitted second time. System can't hold him.
Bold white text on dark background: 'The system can't hold you. Nothing can.'
Stickman at defendant table  -  eighty-eight counts, fraud, classified documents, election interference.
Stickman in courtroom  -  thirty-four felony convictions. First president in history.
Bold white text on dark background: 'The first president in history.'
Stickman at outdoor rally  -  crack sound, bullet grazes ear, blood on face.
Protection detail pushes stickman down  -  he stands back up, raises fist. Crowd screams.
Stickman standing rigid  -  impeached, indicted, convicted, shot. None of it slowed him down.
Stickman staring forward  -  stopped being afraid of losing. Or maybe never was.
Bold white text on dark background: 'That's either inspiring or terrifying. Depends who you ask.'
Election night  -  convicted felon, twice-impeached, bullet survivor. Victory stage.
Seventy-seven million votes counter  -  stickman understanding something about this country nobody else does.
Bold white text on dark background: 'Maybe you do.'
Golden elevator doors opening second time  -  stickman walks through like owning gravity itself.
Stickman at inauguration podium  -  hand on book, seventy-eight years old, second term.
Crowd stretching to monument  -  no wars under my watch. Means it. Probably.
Stickman signing tariff documents  -  stock ticker crashing, Liberation Day declared.
Grocery stores and factory shutdowns  -  Liberation Day doesn't feel liberated.
Midwest factory closing  -  workers blaming someone. Nobody agrees except eggs cost more.
Briefing table  -  red-tabbed intelligence folder, centrifuge diagram showing 60%+ enrichment.
Intel report on desk with red tab  -  months not years. Weapons-grade threshold approaching.
Split-screen phone call  -  ally leader with reading glasses, weekly call, same message: now or never.
Ally has said it thirty years  -  but this time the numbers agree with him.
Stickman signing authorization paper  -  pen on classified document, joint strikes ordered.
Jets launching from carrier deck  -  bombers from gulf, multiple airspace violations, sixteen targets.
Stickman watching screens  -  orange and white explosions, feed refreshing every three seconds.
General clicking pen seven times  -  stickman taps table once. Just once. Silence in situation room.
Retaliation map  -  missiles into ally cities, then military bases, then oil ports across gulf.
Bold white text on dark background: 'Casualties. Real names on real paper.'
Stickman reading casualty folder  -  ages and hometowns. One is twenty-two. Same age as deferment. Closes folder.
Generals seated across table  -  two options on whiteboard: ESCALATE or WITHDRAW. No middle path.
Stickman staring at whiteboard  -  never was a middle path. Couldn't see it from golden elevator. Cool blue wash.
Two carrier strike groups on tactical map  -  Marines and 82nd Airborne symbols. Full escalation.
Largest deployment map since 2003 invasion  -  red arrows converging on region.
Stickman looking down at hands  -  never worn a uniform. Medical deferment at twenty-two.
Stickman at seventy-nine commanding largest military operation  -  contrast with deferment papers.
Stickman's hand gripping armrest  -  fingers tremble half second then close hard. Nobody sees. Cool blue wash.
Map showing strait closing  -  red lines blocking passage, oil tankers stopped.
Global oil price tickers spiking  -  twenty percent of world supply blocked. Gas prices spike worldwide.
Stickman at podium delivering ultimatum  -  reopen strait or energy infrastructure destroyed. They refuse.
Stickman alone with refusal  -  maybe expected it, maybe didn't. Doesn't matter now.
Stickman in bunker week five  -  war map on table covered in red pins everywhere.
Stickman touching red pin on map  -  each one a decision, some this month, some eight years ago. All led here.
Stickman hunched in bunker  -  back hurting for weeks. Doesn't tell anyone.
Stickman staring at torn agreement fragment  -  2018 on camera. Worst deal. Better one promised.
Empty negotiation table  -  no better deal exists. No deal at all.
Bold white text on deep grey background: 'Just the sound of jet engines over sand.'
Situation room  -  word 'WINNING' crossed out on whiteboard. Nobody used it for days.
Stickman noticing the crossed-out word  -  doesn't say it. Silent observation.
Press conference podium  -  reporter raises hand. You promised peace. What is this?
Stickman answering at podium  -  strong, confident, no hesitation. Cameras love confidence.
Stickman alone in situation room after hours  -  cameras off, aides gone, sitting longer than before.
Screens showing casualty numbers both sides  -  going up every day. Not fast. But don't stop.
Cold coffee on desk  -  went cold two hours ago. Didn't notice.
Global economy buckling  -  European allies issuing careful distant statements. Your war, they mean.
Supreme court ruling on screen  -  tariff authority struck down. Domestic economy wobbling too.
Stickman at cabinet meeting losing thread mid-sentence  -  pivots to something else. Nobody corrects.
Stickman recovering  -  seamless pivot. Staff silent.
Vertical split  -  left 'WARTIME LEADER': stickman at command table. Right 'DESTROYER': same stickman amid rubble.
Bold white text on dark background: 'There's no middle ground. There never was. Not with you.'
Wife appearing less  -  sons on television defending him again.
Stickman alone in bunker at 3AM  -  three marriages, five children, but alone.
Stickman picking up red phone  -  holding one second  -  putting it back. Nobody calling. Warm amber glow.
Bold white text on dark background: 'That's it.'
Stickman at command table  -  man who got deferment in 1968 now commands largest army on earth.
Stickman watching jets on screen  -  man who tore up deal now bombs the facilities it was designed to contain.
Paradox web around stickman  -  deferment papers, torn agreement, bullet trajectory, missile trails. All connected.
Bold white text on dark background: 'Something nobody can name.'
Stickman standing in room with no visible exit  -  dealmaker, closer, walks into any room. This room has no exit.
Bold white text on dark background: 'This room has no exit.'
First-person POV  -  screens flashing, generals talking, phones buzzing. Vision blurs then refocuses.
All blurs to white noise  -  blink  -  it's back. Doesn't mention it.
Stickman at center  -  strategic genius or ego without leash? Hundred years to answer. Won't be here.
Stickman on phone with defense secretary  -  listening but part of him still in 2015, riding escalator down, sure everything goes up. Warm amber.
Situation room  -  same angle as HOOK strip 1. Same table, screens, chair. Red phone in same position.
Stickman looking at red phone  -  now knows how much it weighs.
Golden elevator flashback  -  stickman smiling, waving, 2015 warm golden light. Elevator going DOWN.
Bold white text on dark background: 'You don't know the elevator only goes down.'
Bold white text on dark background: 'You promised peace. Maybe you believed it.'
Bold white text on deep red-black background: 'But fire doesn't ask permission.'
